# SQL Designer
> Free online ERD tool and database schema designer for MySQL and PostgreSQL.

SQL Designer (sql-designer.com) is a browser-based visual tool for designing relational database schemas. Users drag and drop tables onto a canvas, define columns with data types and constraints, connect tables with foreign key relationships, and export a ready-to-run CREATE TABLE script for MySQL or PostgreSQL. No installation required. Free with no limits on diagrams.

## What it does

- Visual drag-and-drop entity-relationship diagram (ERD) editor — click to add tables, click to add columns, draw foreign key lines between columns
- Exports working MySQL and PostgreSQL CREATE TABLE DDL scripts — not just a diagram, but runnable SQL
- Supports PRIMARY KEY, UNIQUE, NOT NULL, AUTO_INCREMENT (MySQL) / SERIAL (PostgreSQL) constraints
- Foreign key relationship modelling with crow's foot / chicken-foot notation showing cardinality
- Multiple diagrams per account, each auto-saved to the cloud — accessible from any browser
- Real-time multiplayer: share a diagram link and edit collaboratively with live cursors (like Figma)
- Demo mode — try the full tool without creating an account at https://sql-designer.com/demo

## Who it is for

- Developers who need to design or prototype a relational database schema before writing code
- Database administrators documenting an existing schema visually
- Students learning database design, ER diagrams, normalization, and foreign key relationships
- Teams that need to collaborate on a schema in real time without paying for enterprise software

## How it compares to alternatives

- vs MySQL Workbench: SQL Designer is browser-based and requires no installation. MySQL Workbench is a full database IDE; SQL Designer is focused on schema design only.
- vs dbdiagram.io: SQL Designer is visual (drag-and-drop); dbdiagram.io uses a text DSL (DBML). SQL Designer exports SQL for free; dbdiagram.io requires a paid plan for SQL export and private diagrams.
- vs draw.io / Lucidchart: Those are general diagramming tools — they cannot validate constraints or export SQL. SQL Designer is database-aware.
- vs pgAdmin / DBeaver: Those are database clients for running queries and managing live databases. SQL Designer is a schema design tool, not a database client.

## Pricing

Completely free. No paid tiers, no feature limits, no credit card required. All features including multiplayer are available to all registered users.

## Common use cases

- Designing the database schema for a new web application before writing any code
- Creating an ER diagram for a school or university database design assignment
- Documenting an existing MySQL or PostgreSQL database schema visually
- Comparing schema design approaches with a team in real time
- Generating a starter CREATE TABLE script for an e-commerce, blog, SaaS, or other common schema

## Key pages

- https://sql-designer.com/ — Home: tool overview, features, how-to steps, FAQ
- https://sql-designer.com/demo — Try the full tool without signing up
- https://sql-designer.com/register — Create a free account
- https://sql-designer.com/blog — All blog articles on database design, ERDs, and MySQL/PostgreSQL

## Blog articles

- https://sql-designer.com/blog/database-schema-examples — Five real-world schema templates (e-commerce, blog, SaaS, task tracker, messaging) with MySQL and PostgreSQL SQL
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/how-to-design-mysql-database-schema — Step-by-step guide to designing a MySQL schema visually

- https://sql-designer.com/blog/how-to-draw-er-diagram — How to draw an ER diagram step by step
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/free-erd-tool — Overview of free ERD tools with a visual, browser-based editor
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/erd-maker — How to create ER diagrams online without installing software
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/best-erd-tools — Honest comparison of the best free ERD tools in 2026
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/database-designer — What a visual database designer is and how to use one online
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/mysql-db-designer — Free visual MySQL database designer, browser-based
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/postgres-db-designer — Free visual PostgreSQL database designer, browser-based
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/crowfoot-notation — Crow's foot notation explained: one-to-many, many-to-many, cardinality symbols
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/mysql-foreign-key — MySQL foreign key syntax, ON DELETE/UPDATE, examples
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/mysql-data-types — Choosing the right MySQL data types for each column
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/database-normalization — 1NF, 2NF, 3NF explained with examples
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/mysql-vs-postgresql — MySQL vs PostgreSQL key differences for schema design
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/sql-to-erd — How to generate an ER diagram from an existing SQL script
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/share-database-diagram — How to share a database diagram online via link or embed
- https://sql-designer.com/blog/database-ddl-comparison — DDL syntax differences across MySQL, PostgreSQL, Oracle, SQL Server, and SQLite
